_comment: Real-world scrape targets, one entry per source in config/sources.json (names match exactly so collectors.py can join the two). base_url is a verified live domain. url_template/example fields use {keyword}/{geo}/{subreddit} placeholders -- search-path syntax on third-party sites changes without notice, so verify the template still resolves before relying on it live. method/auth tells you what's actually allowed without a login.

Google Trends.method: pytrends (Python, unofficial wrapper around the public Trends API) -- no API key
Google Trends.notes: Use pytrends for programmatic timeseries (build_payload + interest_over_time), not HTML scraping of the explore page. Rate-limits aggressively -- cache results, see ARCHITECTURE.md §11.

Reddit.method: Public RSS feed, no auth, no API key
Reddit.notes: Verified reachable (HTTP 403 with no User-Agent, 429 when polled repeatedly without a registered app -- both confirm the endpoint is live and rate-limited, not down). Set a descriptive User-Agent header and add delay/backoff between calls. See resources/fetch_reddit.py for a working fetcher.

Pinterest.method: No public free API for search; official Pinterest API requires app review
Pinterest.notes: Aesthetic/lifestyle signal only (credibility 0.35 in config/sources.json). HTML scraping is fragile and against their ToS for automated use -- for the hackathon, treat this as a manual-research source (a person browses and logs what they see into seed_signals.json) rather than an automated collector.

Galaxus / Digitec.notes: CH's largest marketplace -- real CHF prices and bestseller ranks. Verify /en/search?q= still matches their current site before automating; respect robots.txt and rate limits.

Zalando.method: Site search, no public free API
Zalando.notes: DACH fashion/outdoor marketplace. Zalando does run a Partner/Affiliate Program with feed access -- worth checking instead of scraping HTML if pursued beyond the hackathon.

Amazon Bestsellers.method: Bestseller listing pages, no API key needed but ToS-restricted for automated scraping
Amazon Bestsellers.notes: DACH demand proxy. Amazon's ToS restricts automated scraping -- prefer manual spot-checks for the hackathon, or the official Product Advertising API (requires an Amazon Associates account) for anything longer-term.

Ochsner Sport.notes: Largest mainstream CH sports retailer -- the assortment-gap check for the broad/mainstream segment. Verify the search path live before automating.

SportXX.notes: Migros-owned, family/value-oriented sports and outdoor chain. Verify the search path live before automating.

Decathlon Switzerland.notes: Budget-to-mid-range outdoor/camping/sports gear, deep CH footprint. Verify the search path live before automating.

Bachli Bergsport.notes: Premium CH outdoor retailer -- core assortment-gap check (this is the killer-move comparison in ARCHITECTURE.md §1). Verify the search path live before automating.

Transa.notes: Premium CH outdoor retailer -- core assortment-gap check, same role as Bachli. Verify the search path live before automating.

Brack.ch.notes: Trusted, fast CH generalist online department store -- electronics/home/DIY with an outdoor-recreation category. Lower outdoor-specialization than Galaxus; useful as a secondary demand check.

Microspot.notes: Coop-owned, electronics-rooted online mall that's expanded into home/garden/leisure. Minor outdoor relevance -- treat as a weak confirmatory signal, not a primary check.

Manor.notes: CH's largest physical department store chain, online marketplace skews fashion/beauty/home with some everyday sports apparel. Useful for lifestyle-crossover signals (e.g. run-culture aesthetic), not technical gear.

Globus.notes: Luxury CH department store. Minimal outdoor-specific assortment -- relevant mainly for premium material/brand-story crossovers (e.g. a designer collaboration), not a coverage-gap check.

BFS (Swiss Federal Statistical Office).method: Topic browsing + STAT-TAB open data portal, no API key
BFS (Swiss Federal Statistical Office).notes: No single query-string search like the others -- browse Sport/Tourism/Mobility topic pages, or use the STAT-TAB tabular data explorer for structured exports. Strongest credibility in the registry (0.95) precisely because it's official government data, not a scrape.

Gear Media / Newspaper.notes: Editorial coverage for material/brand stories and named-SKU reviews -- this is where the 'commercial proof' evidence (priced, reviewed, in-stock SKUs) usually comes from. Use each site's own search or RSS feed where available.
